title: Dell Inspiron 13 7000 Series i7347 13-Inch Convertible Touchscreen Laptop, Intel Core i3 Processor

screen_failure:
It's been a few days since I had this product and it's a great product when simply functionality is considered. It allows me the opportunity to do work (use a computer) anywhere. The "out the box" settings suggest that battery life is 5 hours; I've only used the battery till 40% (suggesting that total free time is approximately 3 hours).

Although the product greatly improves fun

I love this laptop, it is the perfect size for me and practically an ultrabook with its size and weight. I also love the the screen folds flat on itself so it can function like a tablet. I also like that I can rest my palms on the laptop and not constantly be interacting with the touchpad.

The first one I purchased would flicker every 15 seconds or so, so I returned it. Bought it at a

The features on the computer are very nice. It is light weight and powerful and would have made a good business travel machine.

BUT....

They sent field tech to me and he referred to it as "phantom" touches.  He put a different screen in- not sure if it was new.  Was ok for about 10 months, then the current issue:  freezes during startup and stays on logo screen.  G

I used to love dell products. I used to own the Inspiron laptop when I was in college. It would last me about 3 years before it gave up. I thought this one would be nice and it was cool looking. However, this laptop just did not work for me. I had trouble starting it up. It would stay on the black starting Windows screen for a while until I force restart it using the power button. Once I restart i

Ghost touchscreen, would go crazy all the time. Computer wouldnt boot after a couple months. Dell customer service was great, they replaced HD, mother board, Fan....however, now that I am 30 days out of warranty and it's happened AGAIN. Do not have problems with my other Dell computers, but this one has been a nightmare.
hinge_failure: Within a few weeks of getting the laptop, the touchscreen started freaking out- you get "ghost touches" along a line down the screen, making the whole system freak out, so I had to turn the touchscreen off completely.  Now, the hinges to open and close the computer are breaking after about 6 months of use.  It has a myriad of other problems as well.  I wouldn't recommend it.
storage_failure: When it worked, it worked ok. Bought it for my wife for light use for excess tasks for work. Don't be expected to be wow'd by this computer because you will be extremely disappointed. Touch screen worked well overall but that’s about it. The computer is not all that fast(My $500 Toshiba destroys it). For the money I'd say you'd be better off buying something else. We've had the laptop for almost 3
The hard drive completely failed within a couple of weeks.

The hard drive completely crashed on me after just a couple months. Foursquare told me Dell would take care of it. Dell told me I had to send it in for a new hard drive and that it would take between 10-14 days to be returned to me. battery_failure:
I bought this Dell from a brick and mortar store. It is the 9th Dell I've personally owned over the past 30 years. The other computers were various makes including those I built myself. This is by far the most troublesome machine I have used. I have had to resolve more issues on its functionality than all of the others combined.

Here's my experience:

charging_port_failure: It was doing great until the electrical port fell apart a couple of months in. I was under pressure with grad school and didn't have time to send it off under warranty so not once but twice did I have two different IT guys try to fix it. Both cited poor craftsmanship with the port connection. Both fixes would only get the computer on but not charge the battery. It also would always update every ti

I have this laptop since November and, in the beginning I just love it. But now, 2 months later, I am having some very annoying problems.
The laptop is great, beautiful, not that heavy, and the touchscreen is really very helpful and nice.
But, what annoys me most - and I really would like an answer from Amazon or Dell about this - is that the charger is not charging anymore. I have to ke

The first one I had recieved wouldnt charge or connect to wifi. Amazon willingly replaced it, and the second lasted less than a year. There was a serious problem with ghost touches and was slow. It then would no longer charge so I do not recommend this product.
